attacking_style: Opportunistic rather than explosive. Waits for late selections (final 3-4km), preferring to position perfectly then win from small groups rather than creating gaps solo. Hunts intermediate bonus seconds actively when on GC.
team_tactics: BORA provides conservative GC defense with tempo control when in yellow. Limited domestique support in Classics forces self-sufficiency. Team sacrifices his result for designated leaders (VLASOV leadout shows flexibility).
terrain_preference: Rolling time trials and punchy finales suit him best. Consistent on gravel (Strade Bianche top-5s). Strong on technical circuits. Struggles when pure climbers or explosive riders like ROGLIČ/VAN AERT lift pace on short, steep climbs.
vulnerability: Exposed in echelons and crosswinds (lost 2:05 at Paris-Nice 2022). Lacks acceleration to counter explosive GC attacks despite positioning well. Late-race crashes outside 3km zone costly (Poland, Suisse 2022). Technical descents uncertain (crashed overcooking corner 2020).
key_patterns: Wins from 3-4 rider late selections by positioning/sitting rather than making decisive attack (2020 Paris-Nice)
Hunts intermediate sprint bonuses aggressively when on GC, even countering rivals' bonus attacks (4 observations)
Defends GC conservatively when in yellow, limiting losses rather than attacking (Paris-Nice 2020 multiple stages)
Consistently strong Strade Bianche performer: podium 2018, 5th 2019, final selection 2020
Lacks explosive power against top-tier climbers/puncheurs but positions into final groups through tactical discipline
Vulnerable in crosswinds and echelon scenarios, losing significant time (Paris-Nice 2022, stage 2)
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 showed declining results—echelon losses, failed attacks, multiple crashes, and inability to bridge to top riders suggest reduced competitiveness from 2020-21 peak
